s: Native Android client for Uptime Kuma with FOSS push, no Google services.
d:
URSA keeps every self-hosted Uptime Kuma monitor a glance away on your phone:
real-time up/down status, heartbeat history, TLS certificate details, and push
notifications that never touch Google or a paid relay.

Point it at your server, log in, and your monitors are just there whenever you pull
your phone out.

Features:

* Live status at a glance, updating in real time as Kuma pings your services.
* Heartbeat history, response time, and uptime percentage per monitor.
* TLS certificate details, with local reminders before a certificate expires.
* Push notifications via UnifiedPush (for example ntfy), so alerts route through a
  distributor you choose. No Firebase, no Google Play Services, no relay server.
* Slow-response alerts: get notified when a monitor is up but responding slowly.
* Connect multiple Uptime Kuma servers and switch between them.
* Pause and resume a monitor without opening a browser.
* Username and password login with two-factor (TOTP), and a session that heals itself
  when your connection drops.
* Public status page viewer, no login required.
* Credentials encrypted on device; only the session token is stored, never your
  password. Monitor data is hidden from screenshots and the app switcher.
* Native extras: home-screen widget, Quick Settings tile, app shortcuts, biometric
  app lock, notification actions, and an offline last-known view.
* Light and dark mode using Uptime Kuma's own colors, with optional Material You.

URSA is an independent client and is not affiliated with the Uptime Kuma project.
